methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Mimic Robotics is an ETH Zurich spinout at the Seed stage that has raised $16M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the narrow peer set—firms at identical or adjacent stages with similar valuation tiers—yielded fewer than three datapoints, making traditional peer-median valuation unreliable.

The stage-progression method is applicable here. Using $16M in total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $40M. This method assumes that the company's trajectory and capital efficiency align with median Seed-stage advancement patterns in the sector.

Given the limited comparable universe at this granular stage, the analysis triangulates to a median valuation of $40M, with a low of $34M and a high of $46M. The range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression estimate rather than cross-validation across multiple methods. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the broader peer set, commercialization progress post-raise, and whether the 2.5x multiplier appropriately reflects market conditions. A peer-set expansion or updated traction metrics would narrow this range materially.
